findings.description: The ALL CAPS brand-name writing rule (with its supersession of the Feb 2026 title-case guide) has no corresponding rule in the candidate set. This is a distinct, high-salience governance rule that must be extracted separately.

payload.governance: supersedes the Feb 2026 general guide's title-case rule
payload.statement: Always write LISRAYA in ALL CAPS in all written materials.

findings.description: The supersession fact (this patient-marketing ALL CAPS rule overrides the Feb 2026 general guide's title-case rule) is a governance/versioning statement that must be captured as a facet on the missing rule; omitting it loses precedence information needed for conflict resolution.

The parent-company sign-off placement rule (locked footer; do not add in sections) is entirely unrepresented in the candidate rules. It is a distinct governance/prohibition rule about document structure, not editorial copy formatting.

payload.statement: Do not add the Priovant Therapeutics, Inc. parent-company sign-off within document sections; it lives only in the locked footer.

findings.description: This single rule conflates two distinct polarity statements: a 'must' requirement to include ® on first/most prominent mention with the generic name, and a 'must not' prohibition against repeating ® on subsequent mentions. Consider splitting into a positive placement rule and a negative repetition-prohibition rule for clean polarity tracking.

rule_a.statement: Include ® with generic name on first/most prominent mention in external-facing documents only.

rule_b.statement: Do not repeat ® on subsequent mentions.

findings.description: The scope restriction 'external-facing documents only' is a governance/applicability facet that must be explicitly recorded; without it, the rule could be wrongly applied to internal-only materials.

The trademark pairing rule depends on the generic-name-format rule (® appears only when the generic name, formatted per rule_lisraya_generic_name_format, is included). This dependency/relation between the two rules is not captured.
